skill.name: snippet-manager
skill.description: Save, organize, search, and retrieve reusable code snippets with metadata, tags, categories, examples, and maintenance guidance.
skill.summary: Manage reusable code snippets with tags, categories, examples, and searchable metadata.
skill.author: CuriousLearner

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported command execution, network, filesystem, browser storage, and weak cryptography patterns. Review found these are embedded markdown snippet examples, not code executed by the skill, but some examples could be copied into projects and require security review. No prompt injection or confirmed malicious exfiltration intent was found.

medium_findings.title: Insecure Hashing and Serialization in Reusable Snippet
medium_findings.description: The memoization snippet builds cache keys with hashlib.md5 over pickle.dumps output. This is a copied-code risk because MD5 is weak for security purposes and pickle serialization should not be used with untrusted data.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The exact code uses pickle and hashlib.md5 in a snippet. It is not executed by the skill, but users may copy it into applications without understanding the security boundary.

medium_findings.title: Filesystem Write Snippets Need Path Validation
medium_findings.description: The stream pipeline examples read caller-provided file paths and write to output files. The examples are legitimate Node.js snippets, but copied usage should validate paths and avoid writing outside intended directories.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The examples directly use fs.createReadStream and fs.createWriteStream. The context is educational snippet content, so risk depends on how users adapt it.

low_findings.title: Static Command Execution Detections Are Markdown Fences
low_findings.description: The Ruby backtick detections align with markdown code fences and inline formatting, not Ruby shell execution. No executable Ruby backtick command pattern was found in the skill file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are fenced markdown examples and a directory tree. They are displayed content, not shell execution.

low_findings.title: Network Calls Are Example Snippets
low_findings.description: The fetch and requests calls use example URLs or caller-provided URLs inside reusable snippet examples. No evidence found that the skill automatically sends data to a remote service.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The network calls appear inside clearly labeled JavaScript, React, and Python snippets. They do not include credential collection or a suspicious destination.

low_findings.title: Browser Storage Access Is Educational Snippet Content
low_findings.description: The localStorage examples show cache and preference persistence. This is not credential theft, but users should avoid storing secrets or sensitive personal data in browser storage.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The examples store generic values such as a user object and theme. The pattern is legitimate but needs normal web security caution.

low_findings.title: Key File and Weak Crypto Static Hits Are Mostly False Positives
low_findings.description: The certificate/key detection at line 181 is an object property variable named key. Several weak-crypto detections align with plain text, SQL DESC ordering, or markdown content rather than cryptographic APIs.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines show object-key handling and SQL sort direction examples. No certificate files, private keys, or weak cryptographic API calls appear at those static locations.

dangerous_patterns.title: Weak Hash Example
dangerous_patterns.description: A snippet uses hashlib.md5 for a cache key. It should be replaced with a safer hash when collision resistance or untrusted input matters.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The weak hash call is explicit and located in a reusable code example. The risk is content guidance, not automatic execution.

dangerous_patterns.title: Persistent Browser Storage Examples
dangerous_patterns.description: Several snippets persist data in localStorage. This can expose sensitive data if copied without restrictions on stored values.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The storage calls are direct and repeated. The examples do not show secrets, so the concern is misuse by downstream users.

content.value_statement: Developers lose time finding proven code patterns across projects and notes. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code organize snippets with tags, categories, examples, and review notes.
actual_capabilities: Defines a structure for saving snippets with language, category, tags, framework, use case, and notes.
Provides example snippets for JavaScript, TypeScript, React, Python, Node.js, and SQL.
Suggests ways to search snippets by tag, language, category, framework, or collection.
Includes guidance for documenting parameters, edge cases, alternatives, and related snippets.
Shows a file-based organization model and frontmatter metadata format for snippet libraries.
limitations: It is a markdown guidance skill, not a database-backed snippet application.
It does not automatically index, save, or synchronize snippets across devices.
Some included code examples require security review before production use.
It does not validate copied snippets against project-specific dependencies or coding standards.

use_cases.title: Build a Personal Snippet Library
use_cases.description: Create consistent records for reusable functions, hooks, queries, and utility patterns.

use_cases.title: Standardize Team Examples
use_cases.description: Document approved patterns with tags, usage notes, dependencies, and related alternatives.

use_cases.title: Find Patterns During Code Work
use_cases.description: Ask for snippets by language, framework, category, or use case while working in Claude Code or Codex.

prompt_templates.title: Save a Basic Snippet
prompt_templates.prompt: Save this code as a reusable snippet. Add a clear title, language, category, tags, use case, parameters, and brief notes.

prompt_templates.title: Search by Need
prompt_templates.prompt: Find snippets related to [task or framework]. Rank them by relevance and explain when each snippet should be used.

prompt_templates.title: Improve a Snippet Record
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this snippet record. Add missing metadata, edge cases, dependencies, security notes, and related alternatives.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Team Snippet System
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a snippet organization plan for a team library. Include categories, tag rules, metadata fields, review workflow, and deprecation rules.

output_examples.input: Save this React hook for debounced search input.
output_examples.output: A snippet record with a descriptive title, React and hooks tags, usage notes, parameters, edge cases, and related search examples.

output_examples.input: Find snippets for retrying failed API requests.
output: Retry with exponential backoff for TypeScript
Notes comparing delay settings, retry limits, and error handling tradeoffs

output_examples.input: Organize our backend snippets for new team members.
output_examples.output: A category plan covering API patterns, database queries, testing helpers, Node.js utilities, and required metadata for each snippet.

best_practices: Add security notes when a snippet touches storage, files, authentication, network requests, or serialization.
Keep snippets focused on one reusable pattern and include a realistic usage example.
Review snippets regularly for deprecated APIs, weak algorithms, and project-specific assumptions.
anti_patterns: Do not store secrets, tokens, or sensitive personal data in copied browser storage snippets.
Do not copy filesystem snippets into production without validating paths and permissions.
Do not treat example snippets as production-ready without tests and security review.

faq.question: Does this skill run a snippet database?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance and examples for organizing snippets, but it does not include a running database or sync service.

faq.question: Can it be used with Claude Code and Codex?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill metadata lists Claude, Codex, and Claude Code as supported tools.

faq.question: What languages are covered by the examples?
faq.answer: The file includes examples for JavaScript, TypeScript, React, Python, Node.js, and SQL.

faq.question: Are the included snippets production ready?
faq.answer: Not automatically. Review each snippet for dependencies, security impact, test coverage, and project standards before use.

faq.question: How should snippets be searched?
faq.answer: Search by language, framework, category, tag, keyword, or collection name.

faq.question: What security review is recommended?
faq.answer: Review snippets that use network calls, browser storage, file access, serialization, or cryptographic functions before copying them.
